What it is: The 10 WHY-NOW signals (what moved someone to act), each with its flag, whether it reorders or gates, and how the guide speaks to that person.
How B2B uses it: The gamified entry: 'Ten signals you're ready. Which is yours?' Map your onboarding to these 10.

What it is: The WHO-you-are signals (partner, kids, aging parent, business owner, etc.) that gate clusters and set base order.
How B2B uses it: The first onboarding part. These decide which of your 40 questions a person even sees.

What it is: The moat itself: REORDER + GATE + BRANCH, why show/hide alone is NOT the moat, the lead rule, and why it's deterministic/defensible.
How B2B uses it: The pitch. This is what makes it feel like a 1:1 expert session, not a form. Use in the deck.

What it is: The working template: tag each question with showPolicy / triggerSignals / priorityBoost. Pre-filled with worked examples + template rows.
How B2B uses it: THE ACTION ITEM. Paste your 40 B2B questions in, tag each one. Same schema the engine reads.

What it is: Same question bank, three levers (reorder, gate, branch) driven by two signal families (who-you-are + why-now), producing a genuinely different journey per person, deterministically.
How B2B uses it: Same 40 questions. Every person gets a different, expert-led path. That is the moat.

What it is: Welcome -> Part 1 (who is in your life) -> Part 2 (why now: pick from 10) -> domains ordered by combined signals.
How B2B uses it: Two short onboarding screens set every downstream signal. No score shown, no judgment.
